items.prompt: async def in a cocotb test means…
choices: The function is a coroutine that can suspend at await
Plusargs are disabled for the run
items.explain: async def marks a coroutine; await pauses until a trigger resumes it.

choices: Suspends until a trigger resumes the coroutine and sim time may advance
Sets the UVM test name environment variable
items.explain: await yields to the event loop until the awaited trigger completes.

choices: Registers the coroutine as a runnable test entry
items.explain: Without @cocotb.test(), the runner does not pick up the coroutine.

items.prompt: A plain def test function can legally use await in cocotb.
items.explain: Only async def creates a coroutine that can await triggers.

items.prompt: An async def test with no @cocotb.test decorator…
choices: Is a valid coroutine but not registered as a cocotb test entry
Always runs faster than decorated tests
Is required by pyuvm for every sequence
items.explain: Registration and coroutine shape are separate requirements.
